payload.answer: Extracted three durable architecture decisions and two open questions. No conflicts detected.
citations: Brain DB should be the source of truth.
Cognee should be a rebuildable semantic projection.
Slack should be a strict memory intake agent.
Open questions: Should Telegram be added later?
Which model is cheapest while saturating performance?
payload.decision: Commit stable architecture preferences: Brain DB is the source of truth; Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection; Slack is a strict memory intake agent. Preserve Telegram and model-cost questions as open, not decided.

entity_resolution.reason: No existing entity IDs were provided; extracted entities are named directly from the input.

payload.intent: Extract durable architectural decisions and open questions from the chat summary.

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB should be the source of truth for the Brain personal memory system.
Cognee should be treated as a rebuildable semantic projection of Brain data, not the source of truth.
Slack should function as a strict memory intake agent for Brain.
It is undecided whether Telegram should be added later to the Brain system.
It is undecided which model is cheapest while still saturating performance for Brain-related use.

details: Committed Brain DB as source of truth.
Committed Cognee as rebuildable semantic projection.
Committed Slack as strict memory intake agent.
Preserved Telegram integration as an open question.
Preserved model-cost/performance choice as an open question.
